title: mfa — Eloquent observers

handles: creating → refuse when config('mfa.webauthn.enabled') is false OR the `mfa_webauthn` entitlement is not held by the referring User's tenant

creating → refuse when the credential_id already exists on ANY row (globally unique bytea column; duplicate registration is a compliance signal that either the browser or the authenticator is misbehaving)
creating → normalise transports to lowercase (some authenticators return `USB`/`NFC`; W3C spec is lowercase)
creating → verify sign_count is non-negative (some authenticators start at 0, some at 1; either is fine, but negative is a spec violation)

creating → default device_name when the caller submitted an empty string: `<attestation_format>·<is_platform_authenticator ? 'platform' : 'roaming'>` (e.g. `packed·roaming`) so a user who accepts the default sees SOMETHING descriptive

updating (sign_count dirty) → refuse when the new value is LESS THAN OR EQUAL TO the current value (monotonic invariant — a backward-going counter is a REPLAY ATTACK signal, per WebAuthn spec §5.1.3 step 21). Emits a WebauthnCredentialDeleted with reason='sign_count_replay' + writes a critical compliance-audit entry
updating (device_name dirty) → fire WebauthnCredentialRenamed with the from/to names
updating (disabled_at transition NULL → non-null) → fire WebauthnCredentialDisabled

updating (disabled_at transition non-null → NULL) → refuse (re-enabling a disabled credential is not supported — user must delete + re-register so a fresh attestation is captured; prevents disabled-then-restored credentials from bypassing the retention purge)
deleting → fire WebauthnCredentialDeleted with trigger='user_action' if the caller is the credential owner, else 'admin_action'
deleted (after commit) → invalidate the cached credential list for the Identity (see caches.json)

observers.model: MfaChallenge (proxy — the row is owned by identity/auth's auth_mfa_challenges table; this observer runs on the model class this module exposes over the shared table)
handles: updating (attempts_remaining dirty AND decreasing) → fire MfaChallengeFailed with the reason resolved from the update context (code_mismatch / assertion_signature_invalid / sign_count_replay / recovery_code_invalid)
updating (attempts_remaining just reached 0) → fire MfaChallengeFailed with reason='attempts_exhausted' + emit the `academorix.mfa.challenge` counter with outcome=attempts_exhausted
updating (state transitions to 'expired') → NO event (the 5-minute TTL elapsing without action is not a security event — the caller simply abandoned the flow; only recorded as a metric increment)
deleted → NO event (challenges are transient; hard-delete on TTL is the norm and does not warrant a lifecycle event)

notes: The observer is registered by MfaServiceProvider::boot(). It runs BEFORE HasActivityLog + HasAudit hooks so audit trails see the fully-normalised row.

Every `refuse` case throws a subclass of `Academorix\Mfa\Exceptions\MfaViolationException` — mapped to the errors in `errors.json`. In particular, `sign_count` monotonic invariant violations throw `SignCountReplayException` which the observer catches, records a critical audit entry, then re-throws so the request fails hard (never silently accepts a replayed assertion).

The sign_count check is the primary WebAuthn security invariant this module enforces (beyond the assertion signature itself). It protects against cloned authenticators — if two authenticators claim to be the same credential_id, one of them WILL emit a stale sign_count, and this observer refuses the assertion + deletes the credential so subsequent attempts must re-register.
